ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: Some R functions, such as optim(), require a function its gradient passed as separate arguments. When these are expensive to calculate it may be much faster to calculate the function (fn) and gradient (gr) together since they often share many calculations (chain rule). This package allows the user to pass in a single function that returns both the function and gradient, then splits (hence 'splitfngr') them so the results can be accessed separately. The functions provided allow this to be done with any number of functions/values, not just for functions and gradients.

Some R functions, such as optim(), require a function its gradient passed as separate arguments. When these are expensive to calculate it may be much faster to calculate the function (fn) and gradient (gr) together since they often share many calculations (chain rule). This package allows the user to pass in a single function that returns both the function and gradient, then splits (hence 'splitfngr') them so the results can be accessed separately. The functions provided allow this to be done with any number of functions/values, not just for functions and gradients.

name: r-splitfngr
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = '0.1.2' %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-splitfngr
  version: {{ version|replace("-", "_") }}

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/splitfngr_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/splitfngr/splitfngr_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 9a225e29c2c646e0008a489b2c69f322644bd0b1a08a711448488a5391f1c931

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-lbfgs
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-lbfgs

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('splitfngr')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('splitfngr')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=splitfngr
  license: GPL-3.0-only
  summary: Some R functions, such as optim(), require a function its gradient passed as separate
    arguments. When these are expensive to calculate it may be much faster to calculate
    the function (fn) and gradient (gr) together since they often share many calculations
    (chain rule). This package allows the user to pass in a single function that returns
    both the function and gradient, then splits (hence 'splitfngr') them so the results
    can be accessed separately. The functions provided allow this to be done with any
    number of functions/values, not just for functions and gradients.
  license_family: GPL3
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/GPL-3'

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r
